title: Networks, Queues, Simulation, and Operations Research · Stakeholder Model Mission
summary: A regional emergency logistics group. The group needs a resilient routing, queueing, and allocation policy under competing objectives. Planned artifact: Deliver Regional Transport and Allocation Optimizer with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate network flow in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive integer programming in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute queueing models in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: 26 records meet rule A, 19 meet rule B, and 8 meet both. How many meet A or B?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.
